description_long: This object is a collection of various axes, each with distinct blade shapes and handle designs, arranged in a row. The axes feature a mix of single and double-bladed heads, with some blades being broad and crescent-shaped while others are more compact and rectangular. The handles are primarily wooden, with natural brown tones, and vary in length and curvature, some being straight and others slightly curved for ergonomic grip. The blades are metallic, exhibiting a dark, matte finish, and some axes have decorative or functional cutouts in the blade. The set includes both traditional battle axes and utilitarian hatchets, suggesting a range of uses from chopping wood to ceremonial or display purposes. No visible branding or markings are present on the blades or handles.
description: A diverse set of axes with wooden handles and dark metal blades, featuring both single and double-bladed designs. The collection showcases various handle shapes and blade geometries.
description_view_0: A frontal view displaying the full array of axes, highlighting the variety in blade shapes and handle lengths.
description_view_1: A side view showing the thin profiles of the axes, with handles and blades aligned edge-on, making the blades appear as narrow lines.
description_view_2: A rear view similar to the front, revealing the backs of the axes and the symmetry or asymmetry of the blade designs.
description_view_3: Another side view, opposite to view 1, again emphasizing the slimness of the axes when viewed from the edge.
attribute_handle_type: Handles are primarily straight or slightly curved, made of wood, with a rounded or oval cross-section and smooth grip surfaces.
attribute_blade_design: Blades include both single and double-edged designs, with crescent, rectangular, and bearded profiles; some have decorative cutouts or pronounced polls.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting, with blades fitted onto the wooden handles.
